svg.text: Metatron / Seraph AI Defense - Breadth and Modularity Architecture Map
Broad implementation view with compact capability blocks, selective flow links, and updated differentiators (2026-03-06)
Core app plane: Dashboard | CommandCenter | UnifiedAgent | Reports | Settings | Login | Setup
Security plane: Threats | Alerts | Hunting | Correlation | Timeline | Response | SOAR | Quarantine | Ransomware | Deception
Platform plane: ZeroTrust | CSPM | Identity | AttackPaths | SecureBoot | KernelSensors | VPN | Containers | Sandbox | Browser
B) API Domain Mesh (FastAPI Router Breadth)
Differentiator signal: Breadth without monolithic lock-in
Dense domain coverage is implemented as modular routers + services, enabling incremental upgrades per branch (identity, CSPM, EDM, deception, timeline, deployment).

New represented changes: identity v1 compatibility routes, secure boot adapter compatibility, improved deployment realism, network discovery container fallback, timeline enterprise tabs, EDM rollout governance.
Connector density intentionally low to preserve readability while still showing control loops and modular coupling points.
C) Engine and Service Breadth (Composable Modules)
Each capability domain can iterate independently: adding identity routes does not require CSPM changes; EDM rollout logic evolves without rewriting threat correlation.
Compatibility adapters (secure boot, identity v1, route aliases) preserve client continuity while backend internals evolve.
This architecture favors branch-level upgrades over all-or-nothing refactors, which is the practical differentiator for breadth + agility.
New represented pieces: EDM staged rollout + auto rollback loop, stricter CORS/JWT posture in strict/prod mode, improved deployment diagnostics, expanded timeline workflow planes.
D) Endpoint Fabric and EDM Differentiator
Differentiator: Governed Data Protection Loop (Breadth + Flexibility)
publish dataset -> validate trust/version -> staged rollout by cohort/platform/group -> endpoint detection telemetry -> readiness scoring -> rollback decision
this loop is modular: policy thresholds, rollout strategy, and matching fidelity can evolve independently while preserving control-plane contracts
compared to rigid stacks, this design supports rapid experimentation with bounded blast radius
- Real deployment tasks via SSH/WinRM with clearer preflight errors
- Network discovery fallback for containerized /16 environments
- Unified agent page compatibility for ip/ip_address variants
- VPN startup behavior avoids implicit kill-switch self-lock scenarios
- Timeline UI expanded to visual/correlation/report/evidence planes
- CORS origin resolver with strict/prod explicit origin enforcement
- Optional router import fail-open for incompatible modules
- Secure boot verifier adapter preserves legacy router expectations
- Deception and CSPM page UX improved for operational feedback
E) Data, Runtime Infrastructure, and External Integration Breadth
localhost bind defaults reduce accidental exposure
remote mode still supports distributed SOC operation
Why this Architecture Wins on Breadth + Flexibility
1) Breadth: many capability domains are first-class modules (EDR, SOAR, deception, CSPM, identity, timeline, deployment, AI reasoning, governance).
2) Flexibility: module-level evolution with compatibility adapters avoids whole-platform rewrites.
3) Governance: high-risk automation is wrapped by policy, readiness, and rollback controls.
4) Operational realism: local and remote execution paths coexist with profile-aware infrastructure.
5) Differentiator: dense implementation map with composable branches enables faster product iteration without sacrificing enterprise trajectory.
Design note: intentionally low arrow density to preserve broad readability while still expressing core dependency loops and modular branch evolution.
